headline: A White House order put federal encryption on a hard deadline to outrun quantum computers, even as the Klue supply-chain breach spread to LastPass and the FortiBleed campaign's 110-million-credential haul came into full view.

emerging_trends.text: Washington moved cybersecurity policy onto a clock this week, with a presidential order forcing federal agencies onto post-quantum cryptography by 2031 before future quantum computers can break today's encryption.
A single compromised vendor keeps cascading outward, as the Klue breach turned stolen OAuth tokens into Salesforce data theft that now reaches LastPass and a growing roster of major customers.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue Salesforce Breach Spreads to LastPass

emerging_trends.text: Defenders and attackers raced over the same AI ground, with malicious skills slipping through every scanner on agent marketplaces while poisoned Reddit comments quietly steered deep-research agents.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Malicious AI Skills Flood Agent Marketplaces

topics.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Executive Order
topics.latest_developments: President Trump signed executive order EO 14409 on June 22, 2026, drastically shortening the timeline and setting firm deadlines for federal post-quantum migration.
topics.summary: Post-quantum cryptography replaces encryption that future quantum computers could break. The order requires U.S. agencies to move key establishment to PQC by December 31, 2030, and digital signatures by December 31, 2031, and it leaves national security systems on a separate track.

sources.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats
Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration

topics.title: FortiBleed FortiGate Credential Campaign
topics.latest_developments: ZenoX and CloudSEK reconstructed the campaign's fully automated attack pipeline from a server the operators left exposed, confirming a Russian-speaking access broker harvested 110 million credentials across 430,000 FortiGate firewalls since February 2026.
topics.summary: FortiBleed installs a custom Golang sniffer on compromised FortiGate firewalls to capture authentication credentials at scale. Organizations running internet-facing FortiGate devices should rotate credentials, hunt for the sniffer, and lock down management interfaces.

sources.title: What the Fortibleed campaign means for organizations running FortiGate firewalls
FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign

topics.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited as ICS Advisories Mount
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer reported attackers now exploit CVE-2026-20230, a high-severity server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager, the same day CISA issued seven industrial advisories spanning Siemens SINEC INS, SIPROTEC 5, and WinCC, plus ABB Freelance, Hubbell Aclara, and B&R products.
topics.summary: The Cisco flaw lets attackers coerce the call-control server into making unauthorized internal requests, and the CISA advisories cover OS command injection, weak key protection, and Linux kernel privilege escalation in operational-technology gear. Operators should patch Cisco Unified CM and apply the vendor fixes and countermeasures named in each advisory.

sources.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
Siemens SINEC INS
Siemens SIPROTEC 5 Using DIGSI5 Protocol

topics.title: Healthcare and Utility Data Breaches Mount
topics.latest_developments: Healthtech firm Xsolis disclosed a phishing breach exposing sensitive data on nearly 1.4 million people, Tata Electronics confirmed a cyberattack as hackers leaked stolen files, and Canada's London Hydro disclosed theft of customer names, addresses, phone numbers, and account information.
topics.summary: Three breaches across health technology, electronics manufacturing, and electric utilities exposed personal and account data this week. Affected customers should watch for phishing and fraud and rotate any credentials tied to the compromised accounts.

sources.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach

topics.title: Malicious AI Skills Flood Agent Marketplaces
topics.latest_developments: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 dissected ClawHub, OpenClaw's skill marketplace, finding evasive malicious skills that slip past automated scanners to deploy infostealers and run agentic financial fraud, while a Cornell Tech study showed a 13-word Reddit comment can poison AI deep-research agents.
topics.summary: AI agent skill marketplaces let attackers distribute code that scanners mark safe; security firm AIR's harmless test skill reached 26,000 agents, including corporate accounts. Organizations should treat agent skills as untrusted supply-chain dependencies and restrict what each one can access.

sources.title: OpenClaw’s Skill Marketplace and the Emerging AI Supply Chain Threat
Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
Using Reddit to manipulate AI search results is surprisingly easy

topics.title: Klue Salesforce Breach Spreads to LastPass
topics.latest_developments: LastPass confirmed attackers used OAuth tokens stolen in the Klue supply-chain breach to take customer data from its Salesforce environment, while the extortion group Icarus began publishing stolen data as the victim list grew.
topics.summary: Attackers breached sales-intelligence vendor Klue, stole OAuth tokens linking to customers' Salesforce instances, and pivoted into connected environments. Affected Salesforce customers should revoke Klue tokens and audit every record reached through the integration.

sources.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data

games.result: Mariners 3 · Pirates 2 · Bot 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: Global Tech Selloff Deepens
business_politics.latest_developments: A second straight day of declines hammered chipmakers Tuesday, with Nvidia among the names under pressure as the Nasdaq slid on fears the AI boom cannot sustain its spending.
business_politics.summary: Investors dumped technology shares over stretched valuations, heavy AI capital spending, and the prospect of Federal Reserve rate increases, extending a rout that began Monday and rattling the megacap stocks that drove the market's record run.

sources.title: Stocks Retreat as Fears Deepen About Strength of AI Boom
US chipmakers lead Wall Street slide on rate rise worries

business_politics.title: Hormuz Blockade Strands $125 Billion in Cargo
business_politics.latest_developments: Insurer Allianz called the strait's closure 'unprecedented' Tuesday, counting almost 1,200 cargo ships stranded with $125 billion of goods aboard.
business_politics.summary: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, the chokepoint for roughly a fifth of the world's oil, has halted nearly 1,200 vessels and raised alarm over global maritime trade while the U.S.-Iran accord remains unsettled.

sources.title: Hormuz closure strands almost 1,200 cargo ships with $125bn worth of goods

business_politics.title: Senate Curbs Trump's Iran War Powers
business_politics.latest_developments: Four Republicans joined Democrats Tuesday to pass a war-powers resolution directing President Trump to halt military operations against Iran absent congressional authorization, a week after he signed a framework with Tehran.
business_politics.summary: The Senate's largely symbolic rebuke signals Republican disquiet over Trump's handling of the Iran conflict and the interim deal that freed Iranian oil sales and unfroze billions in funds.

sources.title: Senate passes measure directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran
Senate approves war powers resolution aimed at reining in Trump on Iran war

business.title: Hoffmann Family's Penguins Purchase Approved
business.latest_developments: The NHL approved Fenway Sports Group's sale of the Penguins to the Hoffmann family Tuesday, ending FSG's ownership as Commissioner Gary Bettman praised the buyers' 'great track record.'
business.summary: Fenway Sports Group, which bought the Pittsburgh Penguins in 2021, handed the franchise to the Hoffmann family; Mario Lemieux retains a stake, and Bettman vouched for the incoming owners.

sources.title: 'We've loved Pittsburgh': FSG reflects on its ownership of the Penguins as the sale to the Hoffmanns is approved
1 on 1 with Gary Bettman, who said the Penguins' new owners 'have a great track record'

business.title: Regional Planners Approve $4.7 Billion for Transportation
business.latest_developments: The Southwestern Pennsylvania Commission approved $4.7 billion in transportation projects spanning the next four years.
business.summary: The 10-county Southwestern Pennsylvania Commission, the region's metropolitan planning organization, set its four-year spending plan for roads, bridges, and transit across the Pittsburgh area.

sources.title: Regional planners approve $4.7 billion in transportation projects over four years

business.title: Pittsburgh Weighs Skill-Games Tax
business.latest_developments: City Councilman Anthony Coghill floated taxing skill games inside Pittsburgh, hoping to grandfather the city in before Harrisburg acts, after the Pennsylvania Supreme Court cleared the way.
business.summary: Coghill says a municipal tax on the slot-like machines could bring millions into Pittsburgh's strained budget, following the state Supreme Court ruling that classified skill games under gaming law.

sources.title: Pittsburgh councilman proposes tax on "skill games" to boost city revenue

around_town.title: Chartiers Valley Outsources Bus Service
around_town.latest_developments: The Chartiers Valley School Board voted to hand student transportation to a private provider and furlough more than a dozen district transportation employees.
around_town.summary: The board's decision, opposed by the Pennsylvania State Education Association over job and student-safety concerns, shifts busing for the suburban district southwest of Pittsburgh to an outside company.

sources.title: Chartiers Valley school board approves new bus contract, layoffs

around_town.title: Allegheny County Council Power on November Ballot
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County Council advanced ballot questions letting voters decide in November whether to repeal long-standing limits on the council's budget and access to county-funded benefits.
around_town.summary: The referendums would let the 15-member council expand its own spending and benefits, a move county officials warn against amid budget constraints.

sources.title: Allegheny County residents to vote on critical amendments to council's budget and benefits
County Council lays the groundwork for increased spending amid warnings about budget constraints

around_town.title: PRT Closes Washington Place by PPG Paints Arena
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit will shut part of Washington Place in Uptown for four days for University Line bus-route construction.
around_town.summary: The closure of a key approach to PPG Paints Arena supports PRT's University Line bus rapid transit project linking Downtown and Oakland.

sources.title: PRT to restrict key road to PPG Paints Arena for 4 days

events.title: Pittsburgh Symphony's Summer of Movie Music
events.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra laid out a summer concert series built around film scores.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra is devoting its summer programming to movie music, performing scores drawn from popular films.

sources.title: The Pittsburgh Symphony's summer of movie music

around_teams.title: Pirates Get Good News on Jones, Griffin
around_teams.latest_developments: Tests came back negative for Jared Jones after a line drive struck his surgically repaired pitching elbow, and prospect Konnor Griffin will begin a rehab assignment, the Post-Gazette reported Tuesday.
around_teams.summary: Jones, the Pirates starter hit on the right elbow Sunday in Colorado, avoided structural damage, while top prospect Konnor Griffin moves closer to a return; the update also touched O'Neil Cruz.

sources.title: Pirates injury updates: Tests negative for Jared Jones, Konnor Griffin to go on a rehab assignment

around_teams.title: Beat Writers Weigh DK Metcalf's Year 2
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette podcast assessed how much upside receiver DK Metcalf carries into his second season with the Steelers.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette writers gauged whether Metcalf, whom Pittsburgh acquired ahead of 2025, can post bigger numbers catching passes from quarterback Aaron Rodgers in 2026.

sources.title: WATCH: How much upside does DK Metcalf have in Year 2 with the Steelers?

around_teams.title: Footbahlin Toasts Pat Freiermuth's Wedding
around_teams.latest_developments: On the newest Footbahlin, Ben Roethlisberger congratulated tight end Pat Freiermuth on getting married amid a quiet Steelers offseason.
around_teams.summary: The Channel Seven podcast, hosted by former Steelers quarterback Ben Roethlisberger, filled the news-light stretch with stories and a beer segment, leading off with Freiermuth's marriage.

sources.title: Big Ben Tells One Egg Toss Story That Spirals Into A Sunrise Hike At 11,000 Feet Ep. 131

team_usa.title: USMNT Weighs Rotation Against Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Having clinched a World Cup knockout spot by topping Group D, the U.S. men face the choice of resting starters or keeping the same XI in Thursday's group finale against Türkiye.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino's United States, co-host of the 2026 World Cup, already reached the round of 32, turning the dead-rubber against Türkiye into a question of squad rotation versus match rhythm.

sources.title: USMNT's World Cup success brings dilemma: Rotate the squad or keep the same XI

team_usa.title: Adams Risks Suspension Against Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Tyler Adams said he still wants to play Thursday against Türkiye even though a yellow card would suspend him for the United States' already-clinched round-of-32 match.
team_usa.summary: The U.S. captain and midfield anchor weighed the booking risk against sitting out the group finale, leaning toward playing.

sources.title: USMNT's Adams aims to play amid suspension risk

team_usa.title: Durant to Coach USA Basketball 3x3 Charity Event
team_usa.latest_developments: Kevin Durant will help coach a 3x3 tournament this summer benefiting various charities, USA Basketball announced.
team_usa.summary: Durant, the all-time leading scorer in Olympic basketball and a four-time gold medalist, joins other USA Basketball figures turning to coaching for the charitable event.

sources.title: KD to coach in 3x3 charity tourney for USA hoops

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI industry mimics the trappings of a productive boom while lacking the substance, drawing on his running case that companies like OpenAI and Anthropic burn vast sums without the economics to justify them.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Memory Chips and China, Microsoft and Chinese Models
reading.summary: Ben Thompson contends the big three memory makers may regret opening the door to Chinese competitors, and that Microsoft has strong incentives to lean on Chinese AI models.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport criticizes AI firms for publishing alarming safety claims about their own products, comparing it to a carmaker warning that its flagship vehicle is dangerous, and argues the practice serves marketing more than caution.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
